summary: Large matching-oriented expansion of src/melee/mn/mnevent.c for the Event Match menu, adding static data, menu row/icon/text creation, event-name/time display logic, event menu construction, and input navigation handling. A small vi1201v2.c inline helper rename was also included, likely for local matching/name-shaping reasons. The PR had no visible review discussion in the provided slice, so intent is inferred mainly from the diff.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation work: added Event Match menu statics and several functions for row rendering, selected event text, menu construction, cleanup, and navigation.
Small inline helper rename from inline_fn to GET_EVENTDATA while preserving behavior of GObj_Create(0xE, 0xF, 0).

what_changed: Added <baselib/debug.h> to mnevent.c and used HSD_ASSERTREPORT when allocating MnEventData user_data.
Added new static Vec3 positioning constants, Shift-JIS/string format buffers, color constants, asset pointer globals mnEvent_804A08F8 and mnEvent_804A0908, and text format strings for Event Match UI.

Implemented mnEvent_8024D15C(idx, event_id), which refreshes one visible event row: computes row position from JObj translations, deletes old row GObj/text/icon entries, conditionally creates an unlock icon GObj, creates event number text, and creates icon text based on gm_801BEBA8.
Implemented mnEvent_8024D5B0(gobj, event), which rebuilds the selected event's name/time/value text using gmMainLib_8015CF5C, gm_801BEB8C, gmMainLib_8015CEFC, mn_8022EA78, and SIS text formatting.
Added an inline GET_EVENTDATA(HSD_GObj*) in mnevent.c returning gobj->user_data and used it in fn_8024E1B4.

Reworked fn_8024E1B4 cleanup to use pointer iteration over MnEventData-sized row offsets, clearing gobjs/texts/icons and destroying the menu GObj after the animation reaches mnEvent_803EF74C.end_frame.

Implemented mnEvent_8024E524(event_idx), which creates the main Event Match menu GObj, loads joint/animation assets from mnEvent_804A08F8, allocates and initializes MnEventData, positions the selector JObj using page and JObj translation deltas, creates all nine visible rows, initializes selected-event display, and starts its update proc.

Implemented fn_8024D864(gobj), which handles Event Match menu input: cooldown, back, menu creation delay, confirm transition, X/Y page jumps by nine events, and up/down selection/scroll behavior, refreshing row text/icons and selected-event display after changes.
Renamed vi1201v2.c inline helper inline_fn(void) to GET_EVENTDATA(void) and updated its single call site; behavior remains a GObj_Create wrapper.
smart_moves: Used JObj translation differences between child joints 0xA and 0xC to derive vertical row spacing instead of hardcoding every row coordinate, which preserves asset-driven layout behavior.
Row refresh in mnEvent_8024D15C explicitly destroys previous GObjs and SIS text objects before replacing them, matching menu code patterns that avoid stale UI objects.

Menu construction in mnEvent_8024E524 centralizes the repeated initial refresh sequence: create rows, update portrait/name/details via mnEvent_8024D0CC, mnEvent_8024D7E0, mnEvent_8024D5B0, update animation frame on JObj 9, and call mnEvent_8024D014.

The selected-event display function separates unlocked/time-trial-style display from locked/placeholder display using game-state helpers, making the decompiled control flow explicit even though symbol names remain mostly address-based.
The added HSD_ASSERTREPORT captures the original debug/report strings around MnEventData allocation, which can be important for .rodata layout and matching.
decomp_lessons: For menu decompilation, derive positions from loaded JObj translations when the original code appears asset-driven; this often matches better than introducing equivalent constants.
SIS text objects generally need lifecycle management mirroring the original code: check existing pointer, destroy with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4, then allocate and store the replacement.

When arrays in a struct do not decompile cleanly as normal indexing, pointer iteration over byte offsets, as seen with iter = (MnEventData*) ((u8*) iter + 4), may be a matching tactic for the compiler's chosen addressing mode.
Preserve exact string storage, including Shift-JIS byte arrays, padding, and debug strings, when they affect rodata ordering or match quality.
Menu input handlers often repeat a full UI refresh sequence after each state mutation; keeping that repetition rather than prematurely factoring it may be necessary for matching.
Small inline helper renames can matter for matching or inlining shape even when the helper body is trivial, as shown by vi1201v2.c's inline_fn to GET_EVENTDATA rename.
naming_conventions: Newly decompiled mnevent symbols largely retain address-based names such as mnEvent_8024D15C, mnEvent_8024D5B0, mnEvent_8024E524, and fn_8024D864, indicating functionality was added before semantic names were known.
Static data follows the project/address convention mnEvent_803EFxxx or mnEvent_804Dxxxx for rodata/data/bss-like objects.

Local variable names in the diff are mixed: some semantic names such as event_id, icon_pos, is_unlocked, first_event, page, and event appear alongside compiler-temporary names such as temp_r3_2 and temp_r28.

The inline helper name GET_EVENTDATA appears in both mnevent.c and vi1201v2.c in the diff, but with different signatures and behavior; future work should verify whether this naming was purely a matching artifact before treating it as a semantic convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline wrapper GET_EVENTDATA(gobj) around gobj->user_data in mnevent.c, likely to influence inlining/register allocation in fn_8024E1B4.

Maintained explicit casts through u8* for data row offset computation: row = (MnEventData*) ((u8*) data + idx * 4), suggesting struct layout or compiler addressing was not yet fully semantically modeled.
Kept exact float constants such as 0.035f, 0.03f, 17.0f, 364.68332f, and 38.38772f in UI text creation calls.
Stored packed text colors by writing through *(s32*) &text->text_color, matching the original memory representation rather than assigning color channels separately.
Preserved unusual widened masks/casts in event handling expressions, such as event & 0xFFFFFFFFu and event & 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFu, which may have been required for matching generated code.
Inserted static char arrays for Shift-JIS strings and debug/error text instead of converting all strings to readable literals, preserving byte-exact data.

review_feedback.note: The provided PR slice reports one issue comment but includes no human_text_excerpt, no review_comments_excerpt, and no review feedback examples, so no concrete reviewer guidance can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching src/melee/mn/mnevent.c for semantic renames of mnEvent_8024D15C, mnEvent_8024D5B0, mnEvent_8024E524, and fn_8024D864.
Check whether MnEventData was later restructured to replace byte-offset row iteration with clearer arrays or row structs.
Verify whether the duplicate GET_EVENTDATA helper name in vi1201v2.c was kept, renamed, or identified as an accidental/matching-only name.
Look for comments or commits around mnevent rodata strings to determine whether the Shift-JIS byte arrays were later decoded or named.
